hypothesis: If CHROMA is scaled to larger pretrained Llama encoders (1B → 3B → 8B) and deeper trainable decoders (2 → 4 → 8 blocks), then it will produce multilayer thin-film designs that more closely match target colors (lower CIE76 ΔE) on both paraphrased (TP) and novel optical validation prompts and maintain performance across viewing angles and layer counts, because increased model capacity improves mapping from natural-language descriptions to the discrete material-thickness design space.
